subject: Unused Dependency Audit
body:
## Unused Dependency Audit

**Safety level:** report-only  
**Action taken:** None — report only

---

### Summary

All NuGet packages across all four `.csproj` projects are either actively used in source code or required for compilation via `<Compile>` links. On the npm side, two issues stand out: `isomorphic-git` in the root `package.json` has no detectable usage in any JS/TS source file, and `Squad.SDK/package.json` lists ~52 ESLint sub-packages as direct `dependencies` — none are imported in source code, and all are transitive installs of `eslint` (a `devDependency`).

---

## NuGet — `SquadDash\SquadDash.csproj`

| Package | Version | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.CognitiveServices.Speech` | 1.49.0 | ✅ Used — `AzureTtsProvider.cs`, `SpeechRecognitionService.cs`, `MainWindow.xaml.cs`, `RemoteSpeechSession.cs`, `PreferencesWindow.cs` |
| `NAudio` | 2.3.0 | ✅ Used — `SpeechRecognitionService.cs`, `WhisperSpeechRecognitionService.cs` |
| `QRCoder` | 1.6.0 | ✅ Used — `MainWindow.xaml.cs`, `PreferencesWindow.cs`, `RcStatusPanel.cs` |
| `System.Drawing.Common` | 10.0.7 | ✅ Used — `ClipboardImageEditorWindow.cs` (bicubic resampling). `NU1510` suppressed intentionally in csproj. |

**No action required.**

---

## NuGet — `SquadDash.Tests\SquadDash.Tests.csproj`

| Package | Version | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.CognitiveServices.Speech` | 1.49.0 | ⚠️ Not used in test code directly, but required to compile `<Compile>`-linked files `AzureTtsProvider.cs` and `SpeechRecognitionService.cs`. |
| `NAudio` | 2.3.0 | ⚠️ Same — required to compile linked `SpeechRecognitionService.cs` and `WhisperSpeechRecognitionService.cs`. |

**How to verify:** Remove either package and run `dotnet build SquadDash.Tests` — it will fail to compile due to the `<Compile>` linked source files. Leave these as-is.

---

## NuGet — `VoiceHeuristics\VoiceHeuristics.csproj` / `SquadDashLauncher\SquadDashLauncher.csproj`

Neither project declares any `<PackageReference>` entries. No concerns.

---

## npm — Root `package.json`

| Package | Section | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `isomorphic-git` ^1.37.5 | `dependencies` | ❌ **Potentially unused** — no `import` or `require` found in any `.js`/`.ts` file in the repo. |
| `@bradygaster/squad-cli` ^0.9.5-insider.1 | `devDependencies` | ✅ CLI binary — not expected to appear as an import. |
| `patch-package` ^8.0.1 | `devDependencies` | ✅ Used — invoked by the `postinstall` script. |

**How to verify `isomorphic-git`:** Run `grep -r "isomorphic-git" --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" .` from the repo root (excluding `node_modules`). If no results, run `npm uninstall isomorphic-git` and confirm build still succeeds. The only patch in `patches/` is for `@bradygaster+squad-sdk`, not `isomorphic-git`, so removal should be safe.

---

## npm — `Squad.SDK\package.json`

The `dependencies` block contains **~52 packages that are internal sub-packages of ESLint** (e.g., `acorn`, `acorn-jsx`, `ajv`, `espree`, `esquery`, `flat-cache`, `glob-parent`, `minimatch`, `optionator`, etc.). None of these appear as `import` statements in the Squad.SDK TypeScript source files (`squadService.ts`, `runPrompt.ts`, `index.ts`, `inboxPromptInjection.ts`).

**The only third-party direct dependency used in source is `@bradygaster/squad-sdk`.**

Full list of flagged packages (all in `Squad.SDK/package.json` → `dependencies`, no source-level usage):

`acorn`, `acorn-jsx`, `ajv`, `balanced-match`, `brace-expansion`, `cross-spawn`, `debug`, `deep-is`, `escape-string-regexp`, `eslint-scope`, `eslint-visitor-keys`, `espree`, `esquery`, `esrecurse`, `estraverse`, `esutils`, `fast-deep-equal`, `fast-json-stable-stringify`, `fast-levenshtein`, `file-entry-cache`, `find-up`, `flat-cache`, `flatted`, `glob-parent`, `ignore`, `imurmurhash`, `is-extglob`, `is-glob`, `isexe`, `json-buffer`, `json-schema-traverse`, `json-stable-stringify-without-jsonify`, `keyv`, `levn`, `locate-path`, `minimatch`, `ms`, `natural-compare`, `optionator`, `p-limit`, `p-locate`, `path-exists`, `path-key`, `prelude-ls`, `punycode`, `shebang-command`, `shebang-regex`, `type-check`, `undici-types`, `uri-js`, `which`, `word-wrap`, `yocto-queue`

**Two possible explanations:**
1. **Intentional security pinning** — similar to the `overrides` block (`protobufjs`, `@github/copilot`), these may be pinned to force specific sub-dependency versions.
2. **Accidental bloat** — packages may have been added via `npm install --save` when they should not have been saved at all.

**How to verify:** Run `git log --follow -p Squad.SDK/package.json` to check when these entries were added and whether there was a security advisory. If added unintentionally, remove them from `dependencies` — npm will still install them transitively via `eslint`. If intentionally pinned, move them to the `overrides` block for clarity.

---

## Recommended Actions

1. **`isomorphic-git` (root `package.json`)** — Verify no hidden usage, then remove with `npm uninstall isomorphic-git` from the repo root. Low risk.
2. **Squad.SDK eslint sub-packages** — Check git history to determine if these were intentionally pinned. If not, remove them from `dependencies`; npm will still provide them transitively. If intentional, move to `overrides` for clarity.
3. **No changes needed** for any NuGet packages — all are actively used or required for compilation.

actions.label: Remove isomorphic-git from root package.json
actions.prompt: During a dependency audit on2026-05-24, `isomorphic-git` (v^1.37.5) was found in the `dependencies` section of the root `package.json` at `D:\Drive\Source\SquadDash-public\package.json`, but no `import` or `require` of it was found in any `.js` or `.ts` source file in the repository. The only patch file in `patches/` is for `@bradygaster+squad-sdk`, not `isomorphic-git`. Please: (1) confirm there is no hidden usage (e.g. dynamic require, config files, embedded scripts), (2) run `npm uninstall isomorphic-git` from the repo root, (3) verify the app still builds, and (4) commit the removal with a clear message and the standard Co-authored-by trailer.

actions.label: Investigate Squad.SDK eslint sub-packages in dependencies
actions.prompt: During a dependency audit on 2026-05-24, `Squad.SDK/package.json` (`D:\Drive\Source\SquadDash-public\Squad.SDK\package.json`) was found to list ~52 packages in its `dependencies` block that are all internal sub-packages of ESLint (e.g., `acorn`, `espree`, `minimatch`, `optionator`, `flat-cache`, etc.). None are imported in the Squad.SDK TypeScript source files. ESLint itself is listed as a `devDependency`, so these are transitive. Please: (1) run `git log --follow -p Squad.SDK/package.json` to check when and why these entries were added, (2) determine if they are intentional security version pins (compare versions against known CVEs) or accidental bloat, (3) if accidental, remove them from `dependencies` and run `npm install` to confirm the build still works, (4) if intentional pins, move them to the `overrides` block for clarity. Commit any changes with a clear message and the standard Co-authored-by trailer.
